meta.title: Resolve: Aluar Aluminio Argentino SAIC — Campaign Donations Verification
meta.objective: Independently verify the ARS 5.4M donation figure in the Neo4j graph, identify donor identity (corporate vs personal), recipient parties, election years, and legal compliance
meta.methodology: Cross-referenced CNE aportantes.electoral.gob.ar records, La Nacion, Infobae, Perfil, La Izquierda Diario, Diagonales, DiarioUno, and Argentine electoral law (Ley 26.215)

neo4j_claim.description_in_graph: Two donation records: ARS 4,500,000 and ARS 900,000, party and year fields null

verification_result.verdict: PARTIALLY VERIFIED — amount confirmed but understated; breakdown corrected
verification_result.summary: The ARS 5,400,000 figure in the Neo4j graph represents only the PASO 2019 donations (ARS 4,500,000 to JxC + ARS 900,000 to FdT). A subsequent general election donation of ARS 2,750,000 to FdT brings the 2019 total to ARS 8,150,000. Additionally, a 2017 donation of ARS 3,000,000 to Cambiemos exists. All donations were CORPORATE, made by Aluar Aluminio Argentino SAIC — not personal donations from Madanes family members.

2017_legislativas.note: Maximum permitted amount at the time

2019_paso_jxc.note: Third largest corporate donor to JxC PASO campaign

2019_paso_fdt.note: Initially rejected by FdT (considered Aluar a state contractor), then accepted after Aluar presented documentation claiming no state contracts. Second most important donor to FdT.

2019_general_fdt.note: Additional donation for the general election, after Macri lost the PASO to Alberto Fernandez

totals.neo4j_graph_accuracy: The ARS 5,400,000 in the graph matches the 2019 PASO donations only (4,500,000 + 900,000). It is missing: (1) the ARS 2,750,000 general election donation to FdT, and (2) the ARS 3,000,000 2017 donation to Cambiemos.

donor_identity_analysis.corporate_vs_personal: ALL verified donations were CORPORATE — made by 'Aluar Aluminio Argentino SAIC' (CUIT 30-52278060-6), NOT by Javier Madanes Quintanilla personally or other family members
donor_identity_analysis.madanes_personal_donations: No personal donations from Javier Madanes Quintanilla or other Madanes family members were found in the CNE records or press coverage
donor_identity_analysis.controlling_shareholder: Javier Santiago Madanes Quintanilla controls 68% of Aluar. The corporate donation is de facto a decision by the Madanes family.
donor_identity_analysis.fate_donations: No separate donations from FATE (the tire company also controlled by Madanes) were found in the search results

legal_analysis.applicable_law: Ley 26.215 de Financiamiento de los Partidos Politicos (as modified by Ley 27.504 of 2019)

key_prohibition.article: Art. 15 inc. e) (originally; renumbered in 2019 reform)
key_prohibition.text_summary: Prohibits donations from 'permisionarios, empresas concesionarias o contratistas de servicios u obras publicas o proveedores de la Nacion, las provincias, los municipios o la Ciudad de Buenos Aires'
key_prohibition.english: Bars donations from companies holding government permits, concessions, public service contracts, public works contracts, or supplier status at any level of government

aluar_concessionaire_status.futaleufu_concession: Aluar owns 60.2% of Hidroelectrica Futaleufu S.A., which holds a NATIONAL STATE CONCESSION to operate the Futaleufu hydroelectric dam (built 1971-1976 with USD 474.5M in public funds). 95% of the dam's output goes to Aluar at USD 16-26/MWh vs market rate of USD 70-80/MWh.
aluar_concessionaire_status.concession_grantor: National State (originally Decreto 206/1971 under military government of Lanusse)
aluar_concessionaire_status.legal_question: Aluar's 60.2% ownership of a company holding a national state energy concession raises a serious question about whether Art. 15's prohibition on donations from concessionaires applies. This was flagged by the Frente de Todos in September 2019 — they initially REJECTED Aluar's ARS 900,000 donation on these grounds, but then accepted it after Aluar presented documentation claiming it did not directly hold state contracts.
aluar_concessionaire_status.assessment: Aluar's argument is technically narrow: the concession is held by Hidroelectrica Futaleufu S.A. (a subsidiary), not by Aluar SAIC directly. However, Aluar is the 60.2% controlling shareholder of the concessionaire. Whether this constitutes an indirect concession relationship under Art. 15 is a matter of legal interpretation that the CNE has not definitively resolved.

legal_analysis.2019_reform_context: Ley 27.504 (May 2019) reformed the financing law to allow corporate donations (previously only individuals could donate). This reform was rushed through before the 2019 elections. The reform also banned cash donations, requiring all contributions via bank transfer.
legal_analysis.hedging_legality: Donating to both JxC and FdT simultaneously is NOT prohibited by Argentine law. However, it is politically significant — Aluar was the ONLY company to donate to both coalitions in 2019.

political_context.hedging_pattern: Aluar systematically donates to whichever coalition holds or is likely to hold power. In 2017 (Macri in office), it donated to Cambiemos. In 2019 PASO, it donated to BOTH JxC and FdT. After Macri lost the PASO, Aluar added ARS 2,750,000 more to FdT for the general election — classic political hedging by a monopolist dependent on state concessions and regulatory protection.
political_context.state_dependence: Aluar's business model depends on: (1) the Futaleufu hydroelectric concession (subsidized energy), (2) antidumping tariffs on aluminium imports (recently removed Feb 2026 by Milei government), (3) Mercosur tariff barriers, and (4) regulatory forbearance on its pure monopoly position (HHI 10,000). This creates strong incentives to cultivate relationships with ALL potential governing coalitions.
political_context.kirchner_subsidies: In 2006, President Nestor Kirchner granted Aluar a subsidy of ARS 690,000,000 for plant expansion. In 2007, Kirchner attended an Aluar facility opening to support a gubernatorial candidate.
political_context.only_dual_donor: Aluar was the ONLY company that donated to both Juntos por el Cambio and Frente de Todos in the 2019 presidential election. Nordelta S.A. was the only other company to donate to both sides.

graph_corrections_needed.description: Missing donation: Aluar to FdT general election 2019
Missing donation: Aluar to Cambiemos legislativas 2017

confidence_assessment.notes: The PASO 2019 amounts (ARS 4.5M + ARS 900K) are confirmed by multiple independent sources (La Nacion, Infobae, La Izquierda Diario, DiarioUno, Perfil). The general election ARS 2.75M to FdT is reported by DiarioUno with less corroboration. The 2017 ARS 3M to Cambiemos is reported by Diagonales and a journalist's X post citing CNE records. All amounts can be definitively verified via the CNE CSV download at aportantes.electoral.gob.ar.
